etymology.description: 開(開) (kāi, "open") refers to the act of opening something, which is a core function of the tool in question. 瓶(瓶) (píng, "bottle") is the object specifically being opened. 器(器) (qì, "tool; device") signifies an instrument or implement used to carry out a task. Together, these characters convey the meaning of a device specifically designed for opening bottles.
etymology.interpretation: 開(開) (kāi, "open") describes the function of the tool, which involves removing a cap or seal. 瓶(瓶) (píng, "bottle") specifies the item to be opened, focusing the application of the tool. 器(器) (qì, "tool; device") denotes that it is a device crafted for such a purpose. Together, they imply a functional device for the specific action of opening bottles.

examples.chinese: 我需要一个开瓶器(我需要一個開瓶器)。
厨房抽屉里有开瓶器(廚房抽屜裡有開瓶器)。
examples.english: There is a bottle opener in the kitchen drawer.

examples.chinese: 她用开瓶器打开了饮料(她用開瓶器打開了飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng kāipíngqì dǎkāi le yǐnliào.
examples.english: She opened the drink with a bottle opener.
